title: Pillars of Creation
description: Towering pillars of gas and dust in the Eagle Nebula, one of the most iconic images ever taken by Hubble.

title: Hubble Deep Field
description: Nearly 3,000 galaxies of various ages, sizes, and shapes in a tiny patch of sky — a landmark in cosmology.

title: Comet Hale-Bopp
description: One of the brightest comets of the 20th century, visible to the naked eye for a record 18 months.

title: Crab Nebula
description: The remnant of a supernova explosion witnessed in 1054 AD — a pulsar winds a 10-light-year-wide cloud of filaments.

title: Whirlpool Galaxy
description: M51, a classic grand-design spiral galaxy interacting with its smaller companion NGC 5195.

title: Saturn and Its Rings
description: Saturn's magnificent ring system, made of billions of ice and rock particles spanning 282,000 kilometres.

title: The Fairy of Eagle Nebula
description: A towering column of gas and dust in the Eagle Nebula — stellar nursery sculpted by radiation and stellar winds.

title: Antennae Galaxies
description: Two colliding galaxies stretching long tidal tails into space — a preview of what awaits the Milky Way and Andromeda.

title: Mars at Closest Approach
description: Mars at its closest point to Earth in 60,000 years — polar ice caps, Valles Marineris, and volcanic plains visible.

title: Hubble Ultra Deep Field
description: The deepest portrait of the visible universe — ~10,000 galaxies in a patch of sky smaller than a grain of sand.

title: Orion Nebula
description: The nearest massive star-forming region — a stellar nursery where thousands of new suns are being born.

title: Pleiades Star Cluster
description: The Seven Sisters — an open cluster of young hot blue stars wrapped in gossamer blue reflection nebulae.

title: NGC 1300 Barred Spiral
description: A perfect barred spiral galaxy — a grand design whose arms trail behind it like a pinwheel 110,000 light-years across.

title: Andromeda Galaxy
description: Our nearest galactic neighbour — 2.5 million light-years away, on a collision course with the Milky Way in ~4 billion years.

title: Cat's Eye Nebula
description: A dying sun's last gasp — concentric shells of gas ejected over thousands of years create an otherworldly eye.

title: Sombrero Galaxy
description: M104 — a majestic edge-on spiral with a bright nucleus, dark dust lane, and a halo of globular clusters.

title: Helix Nebula
description: The 'Eye of God' — a planetary nebula 650 light-years away, one of the closest to Earth, formed when a dying star shed its layers.

title: Omega Centauri
description: The largest and most massive globular cluster in the Milky Way — 10 million stars packed into a sphere 150 light-years across.

title: Eta Carinae Nebula
description: One of the most massive and luminous stars known — 100 times the mass of the Sun, on the edge of a hypernova.

title: Perseid Meteor Shower
description: Earth passes through the debris trail of Comet Swift-Tuttle — 100 meteors per hour streak through summer skies.

title: Transit of Venus
description: Venus crosses the face of the Sun — the last transit until 2117. A rare alignment of three worlds in our solar system.

title: Ring Nebula
description: M57 — a smoke ring of glowing gas blown off by a dying star, a preview of our own Sun's fate in 5 billion years.

title: Aurora Borealis
description: The Northern Lights — solar particles guided by Earth's magnetic field ignite curtains of green, purple, and red.

title: Pluto's Heart
description: New Horizons' first close-up of Pluto reveals a heart-shaped plain of nitrogen ice — Tombaugh Regio.

title: Lagoon Nebula
description: A stellar nursery 4,000 light-years away where young hot stars are sculpting gas and dust into dramatic formations.

title: Total Solar Eclipse 2017
description: The Great American Eclipse — the Moon perfectly covers the Sun, revealing the corona and turning day to night.

title: Rosette Nebula
description: A massive stellar nursery 5,000 light-years away whose central star cluster NGC 2244 sculpts it into a rose-like form.

title: First Image of a Black Hole
description: The first direct image of a black hole — M87's supermassive black hole, 6.5 billion solar masses, 55 million light-years away.

title: Milky Way over Atacama
description: The arch of our galaxy rising above the driest desert on Earth — a river of 400 billion stars stretched across the night.

title: Ingenuity's First Flight
description: The first powered, controlled flight on another planet — Ingenuity helicopter hovers above Jezero Crater on Mars.

title: Webb First Deep Field
description: SMACS 0723 — Webb's first deep field, showing galaxy clusters as they appeared 4.6 billion years ago with unprecedented clarity.

title: Carina Nebula — Webb
description: Webb reveals hundreds of previously unseen protostars in the Carina Nebula — star formation in stunning infrared detail.

title: Stephan's Quintet
description: Webb resolves five galaxies in a gravitational dance — four locked in a compact group, one foreground interloper.

title: Southern Ring Nebula
description: Webb reveals a dying star at the centre of the Southern Ring Nebula surrounded by layers of gas and dust it ejected over millennia.

title: Pillars of Creation — Webb
description: Webb revisits the iconic Pillars of Creation in infrared, revealing young stars still forming inside the gas columns.

title: Ring of Fire Eclipse
description: The annular solar eclipse of October 2023 — the Moon too far from Earth to fully cover the Sun, leaving a blazing ring of fire.

title: Historic Aurora Storm
description: The most intense geomagnetic storm in 20 years sent auroras across the globe — visible as far south as Florida and Spain.

title: Total Solar Eclipse 2024
description: The Great North American Eclipse — 32 million people in the path of totality witnessed 4 minutes of cosmic alignment.

title: Jupiter by Webb
description: Webb captures Jupiter in infrared showing auroras, storms, and the Great Red Spot with unprecedented detail.

title: Cosmic Cliffs
description: Webb's infrared gaze sees through the gas and dust of NGC 3324, revealing star-forming cliffs reaching 7 light-years high.
